headline: A Look At GE Vernova (GEV) Valuation After AI Driven Upgrades And US$150b Backlog
summary: GE Vernova (GEV) has been in focus after a wave of analyst upgrades linked to rising gas turbine demand from AI data centers and power projects, alongside a reported US$150b order backlog. See our latest analysis for GE Vernova. Those backlog headlines have played out in the share price too, with GE Vernova’s 1-day share price return of a 1.8% decline sitting against a 90-day share price return of 23.9% and a 1-year total shareholder return of about 1.8x, which points to strong momentum...

headline: Is GE Vernova Inc. (GEV) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
summary: Is GEV a good stock to buy now? We came across a bullish thesis on GE Vernova Inc. on R. Dennis’s Substack by OppCost. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on GEV. GE Vernova Inc.’s share was trading at $847.65 as of March 11th. GEV’s trailing and forward P/E were 47.92 and 59.88 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. GE Vernova has become one of the primary beneficiaries of the global electricity demand surge tied to […]

headline: GE Vernova: Road To $1,000 As Estimate Revisions Keep Climbing
summary: GE Vernova lifts 2026 revenue guidance as gas turbine demand stays sold out through 2028. Click here to read my latest analysis of GEV stock.

headline: Can GE Vernova's Capacity Expansion Power the Global Energy Transition?
summary: GEV ramps up global manufacturing with new investments in Vietnam, Italy and wind facilities to meet surging demand for grid, electrification and power equipment.

headline: Nuclear ETFs in Spotlight as Middle East Conflict Intensifies Energy Crisis
summary: Middle East turmoil and surging oil prices are reigniting nuclear demand, putting ETFs like URA in focus as nations seek energy security.

headline: MIT Leaders for Global Operations announces three new industry partnerships
summary: MIT Leaders for Global Operations Internships at Elite Partner Companies The MIT Leaders for Global Operations (LGO) collaborates with the MIT Sloan School of Management and the MIT School of Engineering to deliver an interdisciplinary Engineering-MBA dual degree program. The two-year curriculum features internships at elite partner companies. Students develop leadership skills for the pharmaceutical, manufacturing, energy, technology, and global supply chain industries, among others. (Photo cre

headline: Jim Cramer: I like GE Vernova very much, they should split it
summary: Jim Cramer got a call on his show recently from “Clay in California” who casually mentioned he was up 900% in Nvidia and 500% in Apple. Cramer’s response? “Who am I to even opine?” But opine he did, and his take on GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is worth unpacking. “I like the stock very much. They ...
